reasoning:
Pierre-Yves Bournazel is the Horizons candidate for Paris mayor, officially backed by the presidential party Renaissance as of October 2025. However, his path to victory is currently exceptionally narrow due to two major factors: his deficit against Rachida Dati on the right, and the strength of the united Left.

According to an early November 2025 Ifop-Fiducial poll, Rachida Dati leads the center/right by a wide margin in first-round intentions (polling 26-28% compared to Bournazel's 14-15%). Previously, there was speculation that Dati’s impending corruption and influence-peddling trial might force her to drop out before the election. However, in late September 2025, a court scheduled her trial for September 16-28, 2026 — six months after the March 2026 municipal elections. This all but guarantees she will firmly remain in the race to lead the right-wing bloc. 

Furthermore, even if Dati were completely removed from the equation, Bournazel faces a dominant Left coalition. The Socialist Party successfully united under Emmanuel Grégoire (who defeated Mayor Anne Hidalgo’s preferred candidate in a July 2025 primary). In a projected second-round triangular matchup, the Ifop poll puts Grégoire and the Left at broadly 42%, comfortably ahead of Dati (39%) and leaving Bournazel as a distant third. 

For Bournazel to win, he would need to miraculously surge past Dati (despite her trial being postponed and her entrenched base) and then defeat a structurally favored left-wing coalition in Paris. Given these enormous hurdles, his chances are very slim, noticeably lower than the initial Polymarket price of 11% (which likely reflected hopes of Dati being legally disqualified).
